title: Home Row: F and J
description: Place your index fingers on F and J. Feel the bumps!
lines: f j f j f j f j
j f j f j f j f
f f j j f f j j
j j f f j j f f

title: Home Row: D and K
description: Add your middle fingers on D and K.
lines: d k d k d k d k
f d k j f d k j
dk jf dk jf dk jf
fd kj fd kj fd kj

title: Home Row: S and L
description: Add your ring fingers on S and L.
lines: s l s l s l s l
s d f j k l s l
sl dk fj sl dk fj
fds jkl fds jkl

title: Home Row: A and ;
description: Add your pinky fingers on A and semicolon.
lines: a ; a ; a ; a ;
a s d f j k l ;
asdf jkl; asdf jkl;
; l k j f d s a

title: Home Row Mastery
description: Practice all home row keys together.
lines: add a sad fall ask
all dad fall lass flask
as fall lads ask fads
a sad lad asks a lass

title: Top Row: E and I
description: Reach up from D and K to type E and I.
lines: e i e i e i e i
de ki de ki de ki
fed jik fed jik
fie die fie die

title: Top Row: R and U
description: Reach up from F and J to type R and U.
lines: r u r u r u r u
fr ju fr ju fr ju
rue fur rue fur rue
rude dure rude dure

title: Top Row: W and O
description: Reach up from S and L to type W and O.
lines: w o w o w o w o
sw lo sw lo sw lo
owe woe owe woe
wore word wore word

title: Top Row: Q and P
description: Reach up from A and ; to type Q and P.
lines: q p q p q p q p
aq ;p aq ;p aq ;p
pew quick pew quick
rope pour rope pour

title: Top Row Words
description: Practice real words using top and home rows.
lines: word rope pour quip
fire wide joke lure
pure disk folk asker
work hard type well

title: Bottom Row: V and M
description: Reach down from F and J to type V and M.
lines: v m v m v m v m
fv jm fv jm fv jm
vim vim vim vim
move vim move vim

title: Bottom Row: C and Comma
description: Reach down from D and K to type C and comma.
lines: c , c , c , c ,
dc k, dc k, dc k,
civic, mic, civic,
come, make, come,

title: Bottom Row: X and Period
description: Reach down from S and L for X and period.
lines: x . x . x . x .
sx l. sx l. sx l.
fix. mix. fix. mix.
next. exit. next.

title: Bottom Row: Z and Slash
description: Reach down from A and ; for Z and slash.
lines: z / z / z / z /
az ;/ az ;/ az ;/
zero/zip zero/zip
fizz/buzz fizz/buzz

title: T and Y — Index Reach
description: Reach up-center from F and J for T and Y.
lines: t y t y t y t y
ft jy ft jy ft jy
try yet try yet
they type they type

title: G and H — Index Center
description: Reach inward from F and J for G and H.
lines: g h g h g h g h
fg jh fg jh fg jh
high high high
ghost the ghost the

title: B and N — Index Reach Down
description: Reach down-center from F and J for B and N.
lines: b n b n b n b n
fb jn fb jn fb jn
bind bond bind bond
brown noun brown noun

title: Full Alphabet
description: You know all the letters! Practice common words.
lines: the quick brown fox
jumps over the lazy
dog and cat play with
joy every single day

title: Common Words
description: Type the most common English words.
lines: the of and to in is
you that it he was for
on are as with his they
at be this from or one

title: Numbers Row: 1–5
description: Reach up from the top row for numbers 1 through 5.

title: Numbers Row: 6–0
description: Reach up from the top row for numbers 6 through 0.

title: First Sentences
description: Type complete sentences with proper spacing.
lines: I like to type fast.
Practice makes perfect.
Keep your eyes on screen.
Type with all ten fingers.

title: Capital Letters
description: Hold Shift to type capital letters with the opposite hand.
lines: Alice Bob Carl Dana Eve
Monday Tuesday Wednesday
Paris London Tokyo Seoul
Hello World Type Fast

title: Punctuation: . , ! ?
description: End sentences with the right punctuation mark.
lines: Hello, how are you today?
I am fine, thank you!
What time is it, please?
Let us go. It is late now.

title: Punctuation: : ; ' "
description: Practice less common but important punctuation.
lines: It's a beautiful morning.
She said: "Let's begin."
Ready; set; go! Type now.
Don't stop; keep going on.

title: Short Phrases
description: Common phrases used every day.
lines: good morning, have a great day
see you later, take care friend
well done, you did a great job
keep going, you are doing well

title: Simple Sentences I
description: Short, clear sentences to build your flow.
lines: The dog runs fast in the park.
She sings a sweet lullaby song.
We eat lunch at noon together.
Birds fly high in the blue sky.

title: Simple Sentences II
description: More short sentences — focus on rhythm.
lines: Rain falls soft on the green grass.
Stars shine bright in the dark night.
The cat curled up on the warm mat.
He read a book beside the window.

title: Questions
description: Asking questions requires a shift of thinking.
lines: Where did you put the red box?
How long will the trip take us?
What is your favorite color now?
Can you help me find my lost book?

title: Numbers in Context
description: Numbers appear in real text — practice them naturally.
lines: I have 3 cats and 2 dogs at home.
She turns 10 years old next Monday.
Buy 5 apples and 4 oranges please.
The train leaves platform 7 at 8:30.

title: Everyday Life
description: Sentences from daily conversation.
lines: Please pass the salt, thank you.
I would like a cup of green tea.
Could you speak a little slower?
Excuse me, do you have the time?

title: Nature and Weather
description: Descriptive sentences about the world around us.
lines: The sun set behind the tall mountains.
A gentle breeze cooled the warm air.
Autumn leaves drifted down the path.
Fresh snow covered the quiet village.

title: About Typing
description: Sentences that remind you of good typing habits.
lines: Keep your fingers on the home row.
Look at the screen, not your hands.
Accuracy matters more than speed now.
Relax your wrists while you type.

title: Mixed Practice I
description: A mix of everything learned so far.
lines: Every expert was once a beginner.
Practice a little bit every single day.
Your fingers will learn the positions.
Soon you will type without thinking hard.

title: Mixed Practice II
description: Keep building — longer and more varied text.
lines: Learning a skill takes time and patience.
Small steps forward lead to great success.
Celebrate each milestone along your journey.
You are getting better with every session!

title: A Short Story
description: Type a short story from start to finish.
lines: Once there was a small red fox.
The fox loved to run in the woods.
One day, it found a golden key.
The key opened a magical old door.

title: Technology Words
description: Common tech vocabulary you will type often.
lines: website app keyboard screen mouse click
browser download upload file save open
email message reply forward delete send
login password username profile settings

title: Top 30 Words Sprint
description: The most common English words — type them fast.
lines: the be to of and a in is it that
for on are with as at this have from or
one had by word but not what all were we
when your can said will there use each which

title: Short Word Burst
description: Short words flow fast — build your rhythm.
lines: go do it at me he to be by us
up on in of or as if an so my
we no am hi ok yes ago big old new
far few fly got had him job key lay met

title: Medium Words
description: Three to six letter words — the backbone of English.
lines: about after again other right place
world still learn every never close
those large often above until while
bring found given hands heart light

title: Long Words
description: Challenge your fingers with longer words.
lines: beautiful important different together
everything remember something wonderful
understand knowledge government question
experience comfortable communication

title: Pangrams
description: Sentences using every letter — a classic speed drill.
lines: The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog.
Pack my box with five dozen liquor jugs.
How vexingly quick daft zebras jump now.
The five boxing wizards jump very quickly.

title: Common Phrases Sprint
description: Phrases you type every day — make them automatic.
lines: as soon as possible, thank you very much
have a nice day and see you later then
I appreciate your help, well done indeed
please let me know if you need anything

title: Days and Months
description: Calendar words come up constantly — own them.
lines: Monday Tuesday Wednesday Thursday Friday
Saturday Sunday January February March
April May June July August September
October November December Spring Summer

title: Mixed Case Sprint
description: Real text mixes capital and lower case — stay sharp.
lines: JavaScript Python HTML CSS React Vue
GitHub Twitter YouTube Netflix Amazon
The Earth orbits The Sun every 365 days.
Dr. Smith called at 9 AM on Thursday.

title: Inspirational Quotes I
description: Great words to type at great speed.
lines: The journey of a thousand miles begins
with a single step taken in courage.
In the middle of every difficulty
lies a great and hidden opportunity.

title: Inspirational Quotes II
description: More wisdom — more words per minute.
lines: It does not matter how slowly you go,
as long as you do not stop moving.
Success is the sum of small efforts
repeated day in and day out for years.

title: Tech and Science
description: Vocabulary from the digital and scientific world.
lines: algorithm database function variable loop
interface framework library deployment host
photosynthesis atmosphere gravitational wave
quantum encryption neural network protocol

title: Code Patterns
description: Programmers type symbols and keywords constantly.
lines: if (x > 0) { return true; }
const name = 'hello world';
for (let i = 0; i < 10; i++)
console.log('typing is fun!');

title: Long Passage
description: Sustain your speed through a full paragraph.
lines: Touch typing is a method of typing without
looking at the keyboard. It relies on muscle
memory to find keys and lets you focus fully
on the text. Practice daily to reach mastery.

title: Speed Drill: Rows
description: Pure finger speed — row-by-row muscle memory.
lines: asdf jkl; asdf jkl; asdf jkl;
qwer uiop qwer uiop qwer uiop
zxcv nm,. zxcv nm,. zxcv nm,.
asdf ghjk l;qw erui opzx cvbn

title: Grand Speed Test
description: Everything you know — go as fast as you can!
lines: To be or not to be, that is the question.
All that glitters is not gold, my dear friend.
The fastest typists exceed 200 words per minute.
Train daily: accuracy first, then build your speed.
